name: Shira-ae (Tofu & Sesame Seed Dressing with Ve
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 1 cup of water to a boil in a small pot and drop in the shredded konnyaku. Return to the boil, then drain immediately and cool to room temperature.
2. Bring another cup of water to a boil and drop in the tofu. Simmer uncovered over moderate heat for 5 minutes, drain, and cool to room temperature. Wrap the tofu in a kitchen towel or napkin and squeeze gently to remove excess moisture. Rub through a sieve set over a bowl with the back of a wooden spoon.
3. Soak shredded carrots in 1/4 cup cold water and 1/2 tsp salt for about 30 minutes, then drain, squeeze dry, and set aside.
4. In a small pot, heat 1 tsp vegetable oil over high heat until a light haze forms above it. Stir in the konnyaku, kashi, 1 tsp sugar, remaining salt, and 1/8 tsp soy sauce. Bring to a boil and simmer, uncovered, until the liquid has reduced by half.
5. Heat a small frying pan over high heat until a drop of water flicked across its surface evaporates instantly. Add sesame seeds and shake the pan almost constantly until they are pale gold and their aroma is released. Bring to a paste in a suribachi or pulverize at high speed in an electric blender with 1/8 tsp soy sauce.
6. Transfer the sesame seed paste to a mixing bowl and stir in the reserved tofu. Drain konnyaku and its sauce through a sieve set over a small bowl, stirring into the sesame seed paste.
7. Stir in 2 tbsp of the sauce, 1 tbsp sugar, 1/4 tsp salt, drained konnyaku, and reserved grated carrot.
8. Serve as a first course or part of a Japanese meal.
